encounter.reason: mood instability on sertraline, evaluate for bipolar spectrum disorder
encounter.location: Portland Behavioral Health Associates, 1520 NW 18th Ave, Suite 304, Portland, OR 97209

screening.name: PHQ-9 depression screening
screening.interpretation: mild depression (patient currently in hypomanic phase; score is artificially low and does not reflect baseline depressive burden)

conditions.name: bipolar II disorder
conditions.note: Newly diagnosed at this encounter. Historical pattern of recurrent hypomanic episodes dating to college years. Current episode hypomanic, medication-induced (sertraline).

conditions.name: major depressive disorder
conditions.note: Reclassified as the depressive component of bipolar II disorder; resolved as an independent diagnosis.

medications.name: sertraline 100 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: taper: 100 mg daily → 50 mg daily × 2 weeks → 25 mg daily × 2 weeks → discontinue
medications.reason: identified as precipitant of medication-induced hypomania in context of bipolar II disorder; antidepressant monotherapy contraindicated in bipolar disorder

medications.name: lamotrigine 25 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: 25 mg daily × 2 weeks, then titrate: 50 mg daily × 2 weeks → 100 mg daily × 2 weeks → 150 mg daily × 2 weeks → 200 mg daily target dose

document_references.description: Initial 90-minute psychiatric evaluation documenting reason for referral from Dr. Nakamura, detailed mood history identifying recurrent hypomanic episodes since college, mental status examination, DSM-5 diagnostic formulation for bipolar II disorder, reclassification of prior major depressive disorder diagnosis, medication plan (sertraline taper and lamotrigine cross-titration with Stevens-Johnson risk counseling), baseline lab orders, and psychoeducation provided regarding bipolar II disorder, sleep hygiene, and mood monitoring.

diagnostic_reports.description: Baseline CBC ordered as part of pre-treatment laboratory workup prior to lamotrigine titration. No results available at this encounter.
Baseline CMP ordered for metabolic monitoring prior to mood stabilizer initiation. No results available at this encounter.
Baseline thyroid function testing ordered to rule out thyroid dysfunction as contributor to mood symptoms. No results available at this encounter.
Ordered to be drawn after patient reaches target dose of 200 mg daily. Not yet collected at this encounter.
